# Phase 2 — Live inference {#sec-phase2}

Phase 2 runs your trained decoder against a live LSL stream and shows class
probabilities in real time. Before you start, make sure:

- The live stream is up and confirmed (@sec-hardware), and
- The `decoder_pipeline.joblib` artifact from Phase 1 (@sec-phase1) is on this
  machine.

## Open the Phase 2 screen

From the running app, navigate to the Phase 2 (live inference) screen. The
screen has three regions:

- a **header** with stream status and the **Start / Halt Inference** control,
- a **settings panel** (decoders, target, and decision settings), and
- a **live probability chart**.

## 1. Discover the stream

The app discovers LSL streams on the network. While it searches you will see
**"Discovering streams…"** / **"Scanning…"**; the expected stream is
`NeuroneStream` (@sec-hardware). Use **Refresh** to scan again if it does not
appear.

::: callout-note
On real hardware the app starts the LSL proxy as part of discovery and reuses
it for the run that follows — it is not relaunched between discovery and start.
:::

## 2. Configure decoders and target

In the settings panel:

- **Decoders** — select which trained decoder(s) to run.
- **Target** — choose the class the readout should track. Use
  **Choose target…** / **Select Target** to pick; **Select all** and
  **Clear all** adjust the selection in bulk.
- **Decision Settings** — configure how probabilities are turned into a
  decision (e.g. the decision threshold/mode).

## 3. Start inference

Click **▶ Start Inference**. The control shows **"Connecting…"** while the
inlet is established, then live inference begins and the header reflects the
active state.

## 4. Read the live chart

The probability chart is a ring-buffered, scrolling plot (rendered with
pyqtgraph). It shows the decoder's predicted class probabilities as new data
arrives. The **● Latest** marker tracks the most recent sample.

### Frozen events

When the decoder fires on an event, it is captured in the **frozen event**
view so you can inspect it without it scrolling away:

- Step between captures with **Older event** / **Newer event**.
- Use the event **filter** ("Filter which events are shown") to narrow which
  events are listed.
- Before any events arrive you will see **"No events yet"**; if a filter
  excludes everything you will see **"No matching events"**.

## 5. Halt inference

Click **■ Halt Inference** to stop. The view shows **"INFERENCE HALTED"**. The
session stops the worker, the receiver, and the stream source cleanly.

## What gets logged

Each live run writes a session log under the Phase 2 live-log directory,
containing the predictions, the markers, a run manifest, and a compressed
predictions archive. These are your record of the session for later analysis.

::: callout-tip
If a run ends unexpectedly, the session can export its accumulated predictions
for recovery — check the live-log directory for the run's outputs.
:::
